frameworks.name: Bayesian models of spatial category use
frameworks.one_line: noisy memory trace combined with imposed prior structure on space

frameworks.name: working memory capacity limits
frameworks.one_line: individual capacity differences predict magnitude of memory bias

frameworks.name: category adjustment / prototype bias
frameworks.one_line: systematic location estimate bias toward category prototypes

core_question: Does individual working memory capacity predict the degree of bias in single-item spatial location estimates?
key_assumptions: spatial memory bias reflects combining noisy trace with imposed prior
working memory capacity varies meaningfully across individuals
individual-level model fits can recover strategy differences
manipulations: individual differences in working memory capacity
single-item location estimation task across participants
key_findings: lower working memory capacity predicts greater bias in remembered location
individual model fits reveal a surprising variety of strategies
roughly half of participants bias estimates outward, not predicted by current Bayesian models
some participants appear to combine inward and outward strategies
interpretations: current Bayesian spatial-category models do not capture all individual patterns
working memory capacity is a meaningful source of interindividual variation in spatial bias
limitations_noted: existing models fail to account for outward-biasing participants
